message:
fix(cli): sanitize terminal controls in routing/info/label error and display paths

Several CLI commands could echo untrusted issue input or label text
back to the terminal verbatim — either through internal/Config error
messages produced by the routing reorder helper, or through human
output of database paths/prefixes/labels. A malicious or malformed
input containing ANSI escapes, BEL, CSI, etc. could move the cursor,
clear the screen, or otherwise corrupt the terminal of an agent or
human reader.

Hardening per file:

- src/cli/commands/close.rs, reopen.rs, update.rs:
  reorder_routed_items_by_requested_inputs() now wraps every
  user-input string in a local issue_input_text() helper that runs it
  through sanitize_terminal_inline before formatting it into the
  internal/config error message. This covers both the
  "returned unexpected issue input" and "did not produce a result"
  paths. The trailing index lookup (requested_inputs[index]) is
  replaced with .get(index) plus a "<unknown>" fallback so a
  malformed routing response cannot panic.

- src/cli/commands/close.rs, reopen.rs, update.rs:
  the bare ordered_items[index] = Some(item) assignment becomes a
  let-else with .get_mut(index), returning a sanitized
  "out-of-range issue input {input}" error instead of panicking on a
  bogus index.

- src/cli/commands/info.rs:
  every interpolated database/JSONL path, mode, daemon reason/detail,
  prefix, schema version, table list, and sample-id list now goes
  through info_display_text / info_display_list (sanitize_terminal_inline)
  before being printed in the human renderer.

- src/cli/commands/q.rs:
  invalid-label warnings are emitted through a new
  invalid_label_warning() helper that sanitizes both the label and
  the validator's reason string before they hit stderr.

Each module gets a focused unit test that feeds an input containing
\x1b[2J / \r / \n / BEL / CSI and asserts the resulting message
contains no control characters and includes the expected escaped
form (e.g. "\\u{1b}[2J").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
